content.full_text:
May 4, 1990

Shirley Wechsler
Treasurer
Senator David Roberti
Ballot Measure Committee
11783 Laurelcrest Drive
Studio City, CA 91604

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-271

Dear Ms. Wechsler:

This is in response to your request for advice concerning whether, under the Political Reform Act (the "Act")1, candidates may contribute their campaign funds to ballot measure committees controlled by other candidates. You are the treasurer for the following ballot measure committees controlled by Senator David Roberti: House the Homeless, A Committee for Proposition 107; Senator David Roberti, Chair, I.D. #891049; Californians Against Reapportionment Dishonesty: David Roberti, Chair, I.D. #890905; and Senator David Roberti Ballot Measure Committee, I.D. #892004.

In its Hiltachk Advice Letter, No. A-89-533, the Commission staff indicated, among other things, that a candidate was prohibited from contributing his or her campaign funds to a ballot measure committee controlled by another candidate. The basis for this advice was Section 85304, which prohibits the transfer of campaign contributions between candidates.

At its meeting on March 13, 1990, the Commission reconsidered this advice and directed staff to advise that such transfers were permissible between candidates and ballot measure committees controlled by other candidates. Accordingly, the Commission staff has revised the advice given to Mr. Hiltachk.

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

We enclose for your reference a copy of the letter to Mr. Hiltachk, dated March 16, 1990, which modifies our original advice.

You also inquire as to the status of Commission regulations concerning whether and when certain ballot measure expenditures are contributions to candidates. The Commission considered a draft regulation on this topic at its May 1, 1990 meeting, but took no action on the regulation. A modified version of that regulation will be considered by the Commission at its next meeting on June 6, 1990. Should you desire a copy of the regulation to be discussed on that date, please contact us after May 25, 1990.

Should you have any questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Scott Hallabrin
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:SH:ld
Enclosures
